title: Besseya bullii
taxonomy: Besseya bullii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. Besseya bullii is native to North America, specifically in the western regions of the United States. Besseya bullii is an annual herbaceous plant that belongs to the genus Besseya. Its classification also includes its family classification as a member of the order Lamiales. Besseya bullii is further classified as a member of the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Besseya bullii, also known as Bull's Besseya, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Saxifragaceae family, native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Besseya bullii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Besseya bullii are teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
